Ad Text:
At CSR, we are building a better future together.

We are a team of 2,600 people across Australia and New Zealand, and we are all collaborating every single day towards a shared purpose - 'Building solutions for a better future'. As a leading Australian Manufacturing business, we are at the forefront of developing innovative, sustainable, and high performing building products and systems.

At CSR, we put people first. We are committed to creating a safe, respectful, supportive, and inclusive working environment that enables everyone to feel valued, grow and truly thrive. We are committed to providing equal opportunity for everyone and welcoming people of all backgrounds, identities, experiences, abilities, and life stage.

 

 About the role:

This is a full‑time 12 Month FTC, based in our North Ryde office for a Senior Business Analyst supporting transformation initiatives, rather than BAU operational activity. The role delivers high‑quality business analysis across complex, project‑based transformation programs, working closely with Project Managers, Change Managers, and transformation delivery teams.

You will work closely with the business to translate customer insights and strategic direction into well‑defined initiatives, shaping work through the lifecycle from definition and analysis through to implementation. The role focuses on understanding current challenges, defining future‑state processes, and ensuring solutions deliver measurable business outcomes.

 

Key responsibilities:

Lead end‑to‑end business analysis activities to define, validate, and optimise business requirements across transformation initiatives
Analyse business functions and processes to inform operational objectives and strategic decision‑making
Develop clear current‑state and future‑state process maps, workflows, and specifications to support solution design and investment cases
Lead the design of "To Be" business processes, ensuring alignment with organisational standards and transformation objectives
Engage and influence senior business and technical stakeholders to ensure solutions meet requirements and deliver intended benefits
Provide strategic insight into business drivers, risks, benefits, and benefits realisation opportunities
Conduct data analysis to identify trends, improvement opportunities, and measurable outcomes
Oversee test planning, acceptance criteria, and UAT to ensure solution readiness and high‑quality delivery

 

What you bring to the role:

5+ years' experience in Business Analysis roles across relevant industries.
Strong business analysis capability, including requirements elicitation, process modelling, and solution evaluation.
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to engage business users, senior leaders, and technical stakeholders.
Proven facilitation, negotiation, and influencing skills across complex stakeholder groups.
Solid understanding of SDLC and delivery methodologies, including Agile and Waterfall.
Strong analytical and problem‑solving capability, with the ability to translate business needs into clear requirements and process designs.
Experience leading analysis on medium to large or complex initiatives.
Proven experience supporting end‑to‑end delivery, including requirements, testing, and transition into BAU.
What we offer

We strive to support your professional and personal growth. Here are the rewards you will enjoy as part of the CSR team:

Supportive and collaborative culture that fosters innovation and inclusion. You will be surrounded by talented colleagues who are enthusiastic about their work and committed to helping each other succeed.
Care and wellbeing offer includes up to 15 weeks of paid parental leave, 52 weeks of superannuation payment, and access to an employee assistance program.
Rewarding career with on-the-job learning, development, and upskilling opportunities within and across our brands
How to Apply:

Just click on the Apply for this job button.

We encourage you to apply even if you do not meet 100% of the requirements. If you are enthusiastic about making a positive impact and you see yourself in this role, we would love to hear from you!

To find out more about us, visit www.csr.com.au

Job Title: Principal Reservoir Engineer & Decision Risk Analyst
Ad Text:
Position Title: Principal Reservoir Engineer and Decision Risk Analyst

Perth Based
2 Year Max Term Employee
Supporting the Subsurface Gas Supply Forecasting Team

At INPEX, our energy is helping to shape a brighter future.

We are proudly supporting positive outcomes in Australian communities, through jobs, training, and social and economic benefits. Our Australian energy portfolio includes Ichthys LNG which we operate, plus participating interests in Prelude FLNG, Darwin LNG, Van Gogh and Ravensworth. We are actively pursuing carbon, capture, and storage opportunities to help provide a cleaner upstream business for Ichthys LNG.

Our inclusive culture is key to our success – valuing diversity and bringing us together as a connected and innovative team. As an international company, INPEX offers unique opportunities to collaborate with colleagues from across the globe. We are keen to attract and develop passionate, motivated, and safety-minded people from all backgrounds who want to help support society’s growing need for secure, affordable, and clean energy.

We are seeking a Principal Reservoir Engineer and Decision Risk Analyst to join our INPEX Subsurface team in Perth on an Employee 2 Year Max Term contract. Join us in helping shape a brighter energy future.

Key responsibilities:

Significant experience and competency in reservoir engineering, reservoir simulation, and integrated production modelling
Generate probabilistic integrated production modelling forecasts
Expand models to integrate new fields and wells in the forecasts
Perform and document thorough QC of inputs and model results
Assess value and trade-off of multiple depletion strategies, development scenarios, development sequences
Forecast reliability review: perform lookbacks on forecast and drive continuous improvement
Prepare technical documents and reports supporting recommendations, business plan forecast, assumptions, constraints, and gas supply strategy
Generate case maps of scenarios to assess
Significant experience in economic analysis and decision analysis
Generate economic metrics from production forecasts
Develop probabilistic decision trees to recommend optimal decision under uncertainties and constraints

What we’re looking for:

We welcome candidates from diverse backgrounds and experiences. You will be a strong fit for this role if you bring: 

Minimum of degree qualifications in Petroleum Geoscience or Engineering
Post-graduate petroleum qualification preferred
Demonstratable experience working in the oil and gas industry on exploration and development projects.
Experience working in a multi-disciplinary subsurface team
Previous experience working on the North West Australian shelf is preferable
Experience of using Petrel, tNavigator, IPM suite, Quorum software

This role is for applicants residing in or willing to relocate to Perth.

Closing date for applications is 22.04.2026. INPEX reserves the right to close applications early should a suitable pool of candidates be identified. Please note that direct feedback on applications will only be provided to interviewed candidates.

What We Offer: 

Leave & Flexibility: 

16 weeks of fully paid parental leave.
Options to purchase additional leave.
Dedication to flexible work arrangements, encompassing variable hours
INPEX Foundation Day, discretionary leave, and community volunteering leave.

Remuneration & Financial Benefits:

Competitive remuneration package with performance‑based incentives.
Superannuation above the standard guarantee.
Salary packaging options, including novated leasing.
Reward and recognition programs, including access to discounts from over 350 retailers.

Career Growth & Development:  

Clear pathways for career development and internal progression.
Structured leadership development programs with ongoing support.
Dedicated study leave and access to learning and development opportunities.

Wellbeing:

Access to wellbeing initiatives, including gym membership options and health resources.
A supportive workplace focused on safety, balance and sustainable work practices.

Additional benefits such as Japanese language classes are available at select locations, including Perth.

We value diversity in our workplace – including beliefs, cultures, and lifestyles, so we can bring our true selves to work every day. If you believe that you have most but not all the requirements outlined above, we encourage you to still apply.

We welcome all candidates to advise us throughout the recruitment process if we can support you with accommodations to make this a more inclusive experience.

As Chief Digital Officer, you will provide strategic advice and direction to the development and improvement of EDQ's business information and supporting information technology systems.

You will:
• Strategically lead ICT planning and integration across the organisation and manage the delivery of digital business platforms to improve service delivery and customer experience.
• Cultivate strong business partnerships and key stakeholder relationships across EDQ and with external providers.
• Provide expert advice on EDQ's data and system structures guiding digital planning and design, and investment decision-making
• Lead the development and implementation of enterprise architecture approach and design, including organisation-wide ‘blueprints', design artefacts, guidelines, and investment strategies
• Work closely with critical stakeholders, both internal and external to EDQ to advance the digital agenda and translate business needs into clear technical requirements for delivery by vendors and partners
• Ensure that ICT Service Level Agreements (SLA) and contracts are in place with service partners and external organisations and that they are managed effectively whilst constantly ensuring that value is being received through these arrangements.
• Provide oversight and provision of expert advice on the delivery of ICT projects within/or impacting on EDQ and ensure that these are aligned with the Queensland Government Enterprise Architecture (QGEA) portfolio, program and project management and assurance policy.
• Analyse and provide strategic advice to the Executive Director and the Executive Leadership Team with respect to the delivery of digital opportunities, business improvements and systems.

Applications to remain current for 12 months.

Ad Text:
Your Key responsibilities:

Lead strategic planning for the Branch including enterprise level solution planning, portfolio and demand planning and the delivery of workable operational plans in an outcome-driven environment.
Lead the delivery of the Branch Engagement Strategy, including effective liaison and negotiation with a range of internal and external stakeholders, including executive leadership, HHSs, clinical networks, vendors, staff and unions.
Lead engagement with stakeholders by ensuring that their needs, expectations and interests are included in operational planning, delivery and evaluation.

Role fit

The essential requirements for this role are:

Demonstrated high level ability to lead and manage both at a strategic and operational level, a business unit that is focussed on supporting and integrating quality services within a performance management and outcome driven environment.
Demonstrated high level knowledge in service and operating models and ability to lead effectively strategic and operational planning for a complex ICT and service delivery portfolio.
Demonstrated experience in establishing performance frameworks and delivering business improvement projects in a large complex service delivery environment.

Why work with us?

Work alongside passionate professionals in a supportive and inclusive environment that values people and prioritises employee success and wellbeing. This includes:

 Competitive salaries

12.75% superannuation
17.5% leave loading

Employee wellbeing

38 hour full time work week
Access to 24/7 confidential employee support providers and counsellors including for immediate family members
Additional flexibility to support your work life balance including access to generous leave entitlements, such as purchased leave, domestic violence leave, reproductive health leave, cultural leave, study and examination leave.

Professional development

Career progression with more than 300 diverse career pathways and career support to take you further!
Access to a variety of programs and initiatives to support training and career growth.
Grow your skills through hands-on experience and access to internal training opportunities and additional financial and leave benefits for approved external training and development.

 Our commitment to equity, diversity and inclusion 

At Queensland Health, our work environment is inclusive and supportive, and we value our employees. We are an equal opportunity employer and encourage applications from people of all cultures, abilities and backgrounds.

Our commitment to cultural safety, equity, diversity and inclusion means we understand some people may need changes to the recruitment process. If you need support during the recruitment process, such as meeting with the panel virtually instead of in person, please reach out to the hiring manager. We value diverse candidates and your need for adjustments will not affect our hiring decisions.
